title: dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica
intro: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica, commonly known as the Iberian Autumn Lady's-tresses, is a type of orchid plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases. The plant's bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, are responsible for its therapeutic actions, which include expectorant, anti-edematous, and antioxidant effects.
benefits: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica has many benefits, such as its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions, thereby improving joint mobility and reducing pain. This plant is also rich in antioxidants, which can protect against cell damage, reduce the risk of cancer, and slow down the aging process, allowing individuals to maintain their vitality and energy levels. Furthermore, the plant has been found to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against various infections and diseases, such as tuberculosis and pneumonia, which can significantly improve respiratory health and overall well-being. By incorporating Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved physical and mental health, increased energy levels, and a better quality of life, ultimately leading to a more fulfilling and happy existence.
actions: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in people suffering from arthritis, allowing them to engage in daily activities like gardening without discomfort. Its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties can aid in wound healing, promoting the recovery of individuals with minor cuts and scrapes, enabling them to participate in sports and other physical activities with confidence. The plant's antioxidant and anti-stress properties can also help mitigate the effects of anxiety and depression, enabling individuals to tackle daily responsibilities with a clearer mind and improved mental well-being. Additionally, its vasodilatory properties can help lower blood pressure, allowing individuals to enjoy daily activities like hiking and walking without excessive strain.
constituents: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica has many active constituents, such as iridoids, phenylethanoid glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Specifically, the plant contains compounds like elatine, elatine glucoside, and 2'-hydroxy-5'-methoxy-3'-nitroacetophenone, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, thereby improving cardiovascular health, wound healing, and reducing the risk of chronic diseases. Additionally, the phenylethanoid glycosides present in the plant, such as 6-O-β-D-glucopyranosyl-tylophorin E, have been found to possess diuretic and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections and muscle spasms. By utilizing these medicinal constituents, individuals can potentially lead healthier lives, enjoying improved immune function, enhanced cardiovascular well-being, and reduced symptoms of various health conditions.
parts: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for their therapeutic properties. The roots of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica are rich in alkaloids, including orchicoline, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and flowers of the plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The medicinal parts of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica have been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, gastrointestinal problems, and skin infections.
preparations: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties. The dried flowers and leaves of this plant can be made into a tea, which is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, and is traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and digestive problems. A tincture of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica is also used as a remedy for cardiovascular health, as it is thought to have vasodilatory properties that help to lower blood pressure. Additionally, the plant's roots can be used to make an infusion, which is said to have anti-viral and anti-bacterial properties, making it useful for treating infections and wounds.
side_effects: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Consuming large amounts of this plant can also lead to allergic reactions, including skin irritation and respiratory problems. Furthermore, using Dactylorhiza elata subsp. iberica for an extended period can disrupt thyroid function and interact with medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. In severe cases, improper use can even cause seizures and other neurological complications.
